vocab.meaning: thuật ngữ pháp lý khó hiểu
vocab.example: I have to read through every line of legal jargon in a contract to ensure I'm not signing away my rights.

vocab.meaning: thấu hiểu những sắc thái nhỏ nhất
vocab.example: When reading classic literature, I take my time to absorb the nuances of the author's tone and style.

vocab.meaning: đọc lướt để lấy ý chính
vocab.example: If I'm catching up on daily news, I usually just skim for the gist of the story rather than reading every detail.

vocab.meaning: sự chú ý tỉ mỉ đến từng chi tiết
vocab.example: Technical manuals require meticulous attention to detail; otherwise, you might skip a crucial safety step.

vocab.meaning: đọc quét để tìm thông tin cụ thể
vocab.example: When checking a train timetable, I quickly scan for specific information like departure times and platform numbers.

vocab.meaning: đọc kỹ/đọc sâu (để học thuật hoặc phân tích)
vocab.example: Preparing for an exam involves intensive reading of academic journals to grasp complex theories.

vocab.meaning: đọc lướt qua một cách thư thả
vocab.example: In a bookstore, I often enjoy a leisurely browse through the travel section without focusing on anything in particular.
I read professional emails very carefully to ensure I don't misinterpret the meaning or the sender's intent.

vocab.meaning: sự hiểu biết hời hợt/trên bề mặt
vocab.example: For celebrity gossip or tabloids, a surface-level understanding is usually more than enough for me.
Academic peer reviews necessitate critical analysis, so every sentence must be scrutinized for logic.
